meaning: adj.譖ｲ謚倡噪,霓ｬ蠑ｯ謚ｹ隗堤噪
examples: The only road access is a tortuous mountain route.
In the intergranular spaces of rocks, however, fluid must flow around and between grains in a tortuous path; this winding path causes a resistance to flow.
Life is but a hard and tortuous journey.
commented another long-time observer of these tortuous talks.
Rather than hammering out tortuous compromises, there were set-piece battles in which the liberals broadly prevailed.
